extraction.one_line_summary: RUBIO Einer attacked repeatedly on final climb to Crans-Montana, outsprinted PINOT Thibaut and CEPEDA Alexander after weather-shortened 75km stage
tactical_patterns: PINOT Thibaut animated break with most attacks but positioned poorly for final sprint, revealing effort-timing weakness at altitude
CEPEDA Alexander cooperated poorly with PINOT in break despite teammate CARTHY Hugh attacking GC group, showing team coordination gap
FORTUNATO Lorenzo attacked early from GC group but only CARTHY Hugh could follow, exposing other favorites' reluctance to commit
LEKNERSUND Andreas lost 7 seconds on steep final kilometers despite being in GC group, signaling weakness on short explosive climbs

rider_signals.RUBIO Einer: positive: won sprint after sitting on PINOT's repeated attacks, showing tactical maturity to conserve energy in break
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: dominated attacking but finished second, suggesting inability to convert aggression into results in final sprint
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: attacked from GC group with teammate in break but gained minimal time, revealing limited climbing form
rider_signals.LEKNERSUND Andreas: negative: lost 7 seconds on steep finish while other GC riders stayed together, exposing vulnerability on punchy gradients
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: extended GC lead to 2 seconds without attacking, showing controlled defensive strength in shortened stage chaos
